brief.speaker: carol
brief.text: Give me the total event count for every unregistered service, across both sources.

template.text:
(
  SELECT tags['service'] as service, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM logs GROUP BY service
  UNION ALL
  SELECT tags['service'] as service, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM spans GROUP BY service
) combined

expected.sql: SELECT combined.service, SUM(event_count) as total_events FROM (SELECT tags['service'] as service, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM logs GROUP BY service UNION ALL SELECT tags['service'] as service, COUNT(*) as event_count FROM spans GROUP BY service) combined LEFT JOIN services ON combined.service = service_name WHERE service_name IS NULL GROUP BY combined.service ORDER BY total_events DESC

hints.text: Carol: 'That includes the registered services. Filter to just the unregistered ones.'
Carol: 'Zero rows. The same anti-join pattern from P6 works here — service_name IS NULL.'
Carol: 'Query error. The WHERE clause filters the outer join — try service_name IS NULL.'
Carol: 'service_name IS NULL — same anti-join pattern as the last puzzle, applied to the combined source.'

success.speaker: carol
success.text:
A hundred and twenty-three events for chrono-portal-mirror. Around sixty for log-sync-svc.

That's not noise. That's two active services, running in our stack, that don't exist according to anyone who has the ability to say so.

She's going to want to see this.
